action: He returns from the east in darkness, soaked through, carrying a waterskin heavy with fresh water.
thought: She looked at me like I mattered.

action: He's finishing repairs to his house roof, using the last light before darkness falls completely.
thought: Shelter means safety. Shelter means I belong.

action: She sits by her hammock as night falls, fingers tracing the rope knots, thinking about thirst.
thought: Without water, we're actually finished, aren't we?

description: Marcus returns from his exploration soaked and carrying water. Jade is at her hammock, and in the gathering dark, they talk—his matter-of-fact discovery and her quiet gratitude slowly breaking through his walls.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I was starting to worry. You've been gone all day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Went exploring. Found something.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What? You look soaked.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water. Freshwater spring, east side, past the rocks.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're serious? We have water?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No, I—sorry. That came out wrong. How did you even find it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Listened. Most things flow downhill. Took a while.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You did this alone. At night. For us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For you, specifically? No. For water, yes.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Don't do that. Don't push it away.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not pushing anything. I'm being honest.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You could have told Ryan. Let him help.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I work better alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. But you didn't have to.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You did.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Did what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Need it. Water. So I found it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: There's something else you're not saying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Maybe I just wanted to be useful.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're useful. You always are.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Doesn't feel like it here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm just... not used to this. Being around people.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're doing fine.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You don't have to say that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not saying it to be nice. You found water when we were all panicking.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Ryan wasn't panicking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No, but I was. And you noticed.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I noticed.

action: He wakes before dawn and walks to the water source, moving through the semi-darkness with practiced ease.
thought: She said the water helped. That matters.

action: He emerges from his house as the light rises, stretching and already thinking about what he can build.
thought: I feel something good is building today.

action: She wakes in her hammock to sunlight filtering through the canopy, thinking of Marcus and how he brought her water.
thought: He brought water. I can't get past him.

description: All three arrive at the water source in the cool morning, and a conversation unfolds about gathering, trust, and what comes next.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Morning! Good to see the sun coming up properly.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water's good today.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, was just thinking about that. We're going to need a lot more if we want to—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Hi. Sorry, am I interrupting something?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: No, no, of course not. Come on, fill up while it's cool.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's cold this morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It is. I was thinking... about something you said last night, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That water's everything. You were right. I didn't sleep properly until I drank.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He's good like that. Marcus knows what matters.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Just observation.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Well, I want to set up a better system for collection. Maybe a trap, or—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: A trap sounds smart. There was rain yesterday, so—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: North side. More runoff.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: North side, yeah okay. Marcus, you want to help me scout?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: After water.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I could help too, if you want.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The more the better. It's going to be a lot of work.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're quiet this morning.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Always quiet.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He's reflective. I think that's good. Better than me running my mouth all the time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No, I like it. It feels... honest.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Mm.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We should get going soon though. We're burning daylight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I'm ready.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Ready.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Great. Let's head north then. Jade, you'll see the valley I was talking about?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. I'm excited to see what you're building.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: This is good. All three of us working together, it feels—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Like we might actually make it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: One day at a time.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: One day at a time. I'll take it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come on then.

action: Repairs the frame of his tent with branches gathered from the island, working methodically in the midday heat.
thought: She was at the water source with Ryan. Again. Not that I'm counting.

action: Approaches Jade at the water source with a warm greeting, already thinking of reasons to work beside her.
thought: She's genuinely happy to see me. That has to mean something, doesn't it?

action: Sits by the water collecting some for storage, her attention drifting between the work and the presence of both men.
thought: Everyone is building something. Building meaning, building shelter, building plans. I'm just here.

description: Ryan finds Jade at the water source midday, and they fall into easy conversation while gathering water and fallen branches. Marcus works quietly on his tent nearby, within sight but not part of their exchange.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hey. You've been up long?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not really, just thinking. About all this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Thinking good or thinking hard?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Too early to tell. Maybe both.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fair. Listen, I was going to gather more wood before it gets too hot. Want to help?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why are you asking me?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Why not? You're good company. And honestly, I like talking to you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. You actually listen. Most people just wait for their turn to talk.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that rare?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: More than it should be. Come on, there's a good spot near the clearing. Not far.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Marcus mentioned a clearing.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He did?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yesterday when we all talked. This is it, right?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, he's got good instincts for where things are. For people too, I think.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't know. He notices things. You can feel it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You like him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I do, actually. He's solid. The kind of person you want nearby when something goes wrong.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He brought us water. That mattered.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It did. More than solving a problem, I think. It showed something.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Showed what?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That he cares. Even if he doesn't know how to say it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I can feel that in him. It's strange.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Strange how?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: A man like that, so closed off, but you can still feel the care. It's there underneath.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You really see him, don't you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do I? I don't know. Maybe I'm just paying attention.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's seeing. Come on, let's grab some wood. I'll help with the bigger pieces.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. Lead the way.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We'll come back before it gets dark. That way everyone knows where we are.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Does that matter?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I think so. We're three people on an island. Right now, knowing where everyone is matters.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that about safety or something else?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe both.

action: Marcus reinforces his tent frame with salvaged driftwood, working methodically as the midday sun beats down.
thought: She keeps finding reasons to be near him.

action: Ryan hears a sound from the beach while gathering branches and hurries to find Judy collapsed on the sand, barely conscious.
thought: Someone's in real trouble. I can help.

action: Jade works on gathering coconuts when she hears Ryan's voice calling from the beach; she abandons her task and moves toward him.
thought: Something has changed. I feel it.

action: Judy washes up on the sand after hours of swimming, her body shutting down from exhaustion and dehydration until she hears voices approaching.
thought: Please let them be real. Please.

description: Ryan finds Judy collapsed on the beach after swimming from a plane crash offshore and brings her to Jade at the water source; the three meet for the first time as Judy slowly realizes she's found other people.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hey—are you alright? Where did you come from?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I swam. The plane went down and I just... I couldn't stop swimming.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: A plane crash? When was this? How long were you in the water?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't know. Hours and hours and I lost count of everything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. You're safe now. What's your name?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Judy. Judy Chen. There was someone with me but the current was too strong and he didn't make it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm deeply sorry about that. But you did. You survived, Judy. That's extraordinary.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Ryan, who's this? Is everything okay?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Jade, this is Judy. She was in a plane crash offshore and managed to swim here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Judy, come sit down. You look exhausted. Here, take my hand.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm sorry to just appear here like this. I'm so sorry.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Don't be sorry. You're here now. That's what matters.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We found a fresh water source this morning. And we have shelter and food.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You're just... helping me? Both of you, without even knowing me?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of course we are. Why would we not?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because it's who we are. Because you need help. That's the only reason we need.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I thought I was going to die out there. I really thought I wasn't going to make it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You didn't die, though. You're here and you're alive.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How far along are you? Is that okay to ask?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Eight months. Nearly there. The baby kept moving while I was swimming and that's what kept me going.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The baby is safe now. You both are.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: There's another person here too. Marcus. He's staying on the east side but he's capable and good. He'll want to help.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: So there are... four of us now? Four people?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yes. Four of us on this island. That's not nothing.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Let's get you to the firepit where it's warm and we can tend to you properly.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't want to just take. I need to contribute somehow. I need to pull my weight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: First, you rest. You eat, you drink, you sleep. Everything else comes after.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm alive. I really made it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You did. Come on.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We'll get you some water first, then food. Just small amounts at first, okay?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. Yes. I can do that.

action: He gathered firewood as dusk fell, then positioned himself at the edge of the firepit, watching the group settle.
thought: I can't look away from this.

action: He brought Judy from the beach to the firepit, realized she had nowhere safe to sleep, and offered her his house without hesitation.
thought: She's trusting me with everything right now.

action: She watched the group gather as evening deepened and began helping gather what food and water they had to share with Judy.
thought: He's going to break his heart, I think.

action: She was brought from the beach to the firepit, and then Ryan offered her shelter in his house for the night—the first real roof over her head since the crash.
thought: Safe. Finally. The baby is safe.

description: Evening at the firepit and near the house: Ryan brings Judy from the beach and realizes she has nowhere safe to sleep. A conversation unfolds about shelter, food, trust, and belonging.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Judy, come sit by the fire a minute. You look completely drained.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm okay. Really. I don't want to be any trouble.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not trouble. You're hungry and exhausted.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And you can't stay on the beach tonight. It gets cold, and you need shelter.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's right. You need a real place to sleep.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I've managed before. I can—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not tonight you can't. Look at yourself.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: My house has room. You're staying there. End of discussion.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But where will you sleep?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Don't worry about that. My place is yours tonight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Exactly. We figure out the rest in the morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And I can help you get comfortable. We'll make it nice.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm eight months pregnant. I just swam to an island. I don't...

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You did more than enough to survive. Now you rest.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How long has it been since you've eaten?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't remember. Maybe this morning? I forgot after...

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay, we're feeding you first. Then sleep.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water too. You need water.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Let's go inside. I'll get you some fruit and coconut.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't want to take your things. You need them.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We have enough. Don't worry about that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And you're not just our responsibility—you're one of us now. That's how this works.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: One of us?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. You're one of us. You got here. You're alive. You're in.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Come on. Stop arguing and let us help.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We'll get you settled, and tomorrow we figure out something more permanent.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Thank you. I don't know how to say thank you enough.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You don't need to say anything. Just get some rest.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're one of four people now, on an island, who all need each other. That changes things.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll keep the fire going. You two get her inside.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And we'll make sure she's warm. That's what matters.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Will you be okay out here? It's cold.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I've got my tent. I'm fine.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come on, Judy. Let's go.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We've got you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Tomorrow, when you've rested, you'll feel different. I promise.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. Yes. I can do this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You already did the hard part.

action: Returns to the firepit after dark and settles back, arms crossed, watching the stray and the others with careful eyes.
thought: What is it doing here? Why now?

action: Kneels gently before the stray, offering food and water with hands that shake slightly from emotion and determination.
thought: Something needs me. I can help it.

action: Sits near Ryan and the stray, speaking in soft tones, her expression softened by the firelight and empathy.
thought: It's like us—alone, terrified, needing home.

action: Watches the stray with intense protectiveness, tears streaming quietly, one hand resting on her belly, seeing her own survival in the lost puppy.
thought: We're both broken. We're both surviving somehow.

action: Creeps from the darkness of the beach toward the firelight, trembling and soaked, drawn by warmth and the desperate hope of salvation.
thought: Warm. Safe. Is it safe? Please.

description: At the firepit on night of Day 2, as darkness falls, a soaked brown puppy emerges from the beach toward the warmth of the fire. Ryan, Jade, and Judy encounter the stray in a moment that will change everything. Marcus watches from the edge, skeptical and calculating.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Is that... is that a dog at the edge of the fire?

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [shivering, wet fur matted, creeping closer to the warmth]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Oh god. Look at it. It's so thin. So scared.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: It's just a puppy. Just a scared little puppy.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's starving. Look at its ribs. We have food, right?

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [whimpers, ears back, stopping ten feet away]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Let it come to us. Slow movements. It's terrified.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hey, buddy. It's okay. You're safe here now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Oh sweetheart. Oh god, you poor thing. We'll take care of you.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [nose twitching, approaching Ryan's outstretched hand]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look at him. He's desperate for help but so afraid.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Here. Just some fish. Small pieces. That's okay, buddy.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [eats tentatively from Ryan's palm, trembling, eyes wet]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Look at how hungry it is. How did it survive alone out there?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It didn't. It found us. That's what matters now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It needs water more than food. Let me get a bowl.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [whines softly, tail moving slightly, watching Ryan leave]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: He's coming back. Stay with us. You're not alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're going to get you through this. All of us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: [returns with water] Here. Drink, little one. Easy.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [drinks deeply from the bowl, eyes closing, finally believing]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Can it stay? Can it sleep by the fire with us?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of course it stays. It's ours now. We're its family.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tomorrow we'll build it a shelter. For now, by the fire is good.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [curls up slowly beside the fire, head dropping, safe at last]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look. It's actually sleeping. It trusts us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: We'll keep you safe. I promise. You're not alone anymore.

action: Wakes early in his tent and lies there for a long moment, his mind elsewhere, before rising to check the fire.
thought: I can't stop thinking about her hand.

action: Wakes beside Judy in the house, realizing he's slept deeply but still feels hollowed out; lies there gathering strength.
thought: How long can I keep this up?

action: Wakes in her hammock with unusual energy and heads into the jungle edge to gather firewood.
thought: The morning light feels like a gift.

action: Wakes in the safety of the house and sits up slowly, one hand on her belly, listening for sounds of the others.
thought: Another day. He didn't leave me.

action: Wakes under the palms where Ryan left it the night before, stretches uncertainly, and watches the beach path for movement.
thought: Will they come back? Am I safe?

description: At dusk, Jade finds Marcus alone at the cliff overlooking the ocean, his composure beginning to crack; in the gathering darkness, a moment of genuine vulnerability unfolds between them.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Since we put the puppy down for the night, you've been out here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's a good spot. Quiet.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You've been crying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: My eyes sweat when I stare at the horizon.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You, breaking. Just for a moment.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm fine, Jade.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Stop saying that. You're not fine and neither am I.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What do you want from me?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The truth. Why are you really out here?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I came to the island to disappear. Not like this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not with people, you mean.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not with anyone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And now you're stuck with us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Now I'm stuck with you. That's different.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that bad?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't know. It scares me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because I don't know how to let anyone close.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're letting me close right now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Jade...

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Just sit with me. You don't have to explain anything.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I used to think I was better alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And now?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Now I'm not sure what I want.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We have time to figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Do we?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yes. We're not going anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I think I needed this. You. This.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know.

action: He sits at the cliff lookout, throwing stones into the ocean one by one, each one an attempt to settle something restless.
thought: Tonight. What do I say tonight?

action: He sits near the firepit with the stray's head resting against his leg, slowly hand-feeding it scraps of dried fish, watching its shoulders relax.
thought: It's starting to trust me. That matters.

action: She approaches from the jungle path carrying an armful of coconuts and fresh palm fronds, moving with the ease of someone who belongs everywhere and nowhere.
thought: The light is perfect right now. Golden.

action: She emerges from the house door, one hand pressed to the small of her back, drawn by the sound of voices and the sight of Ryan tending something gentle.
thought: He feeds it so gently. Like he cares.

action: The puppy leans fully into Ryan's leg, its tail lifting slightly, testing whether the warmth and kindness are real or another false safety that might vanish.
thought: His hand is warm. Is this staying?

description: At the firepit during midday, Ryan hand-feeds the recovering stray while Jade arrives with gathered coconuts and Judy watches from the porch, uncertain whether to join or stay apart.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Here, slow. You're safe. Just take it easy.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [soft whimper, sniffs the fish, takes it gently]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How much is it eating now? It looks less scared than yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: A little more each time. I think it's starting to understand we're not going to hurt it.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Can I... do you want help? I could sit with it?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, you can. Just keep your movements slow. It spooks easy.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [ears perk up, glances at Judy but stays against Ryan's leg]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It bonded with you first because you weren't afraid. Animals know that stuff.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm not sure what I was afraid of. Still am, kind of. Like if I stop moving, I'll just disappear.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You won't. You're here. You're real.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's why it trusts him. He actually shows up.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah well, showing up is the easy part. Staying—that's what matters, right?

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [tail wags once, then settles]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about what you said last night. About how something needs you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't remember saying that exactly, but... yeah. It matters to be needed.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Everyone here needs something. That's kind of why we're here, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe. Or maybe we're all just people who don't fit anywhere else, so the island fits us somehow.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Do you think we'll ever leave?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I think we'll stay as long as we need to. Then we'll know when to go.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [rests head fully on Ryan's thigh, a small sigh]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You know, Judy—the puppy came here because it was desperate, and it found the warmest place. That's what we're all doing.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I wasn't that brave. I didn't choose this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Neither did it. But you both survived. That's the same thing, kind of.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The island doesn't care how you got here. Just that you did.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [tail thumps against the ground, eyes drifting closed]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: It's falling asleep. It really does feel safe with you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're not safe. None of us are. But we're together. Maybe that's the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's sweet, Ryan. And probably true.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [soft snore, paws twitch in dream]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I hope Marcus sees this. Maybe he'd understand why we're keeping it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Marcus doesn't need to understand. He just needs to let us try.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He will. Marcus is harder on himself than anyone else. The rest of us he just watches.

action: He remains at the cliff edge as twilight deepens, watching the firelight flicker across the water, words unsaid building in his chest.
thought: She's with him at the fire.

action: He sits by the fire with the stray against his leg, his exhaustion easing as Jade settles beside him with fresh kindling.
thought: Maybe I can keep going if I'm not alone.

action: She arrives with an armload of dry wood and settles by the firepit, offering Ryan what he doesn't ask for but desperately needs.
thought: He's carrying too much on his own.

action: She sits alone on the house porch, watching Ryan and Jade by the firelight, her hand on her belly, her chest tight with something she can't name.
thought: He's different when she's near him.

action: The puppy eats from Ryan's hand and settles against his leg, tail slow and steady, the terror of the past days finally giving way to trust and sleep.
thought: Safe. This is safe now.

description: By the firepit at evening, Ryan feeds the recovering stray while Jade arrives with firewood, and they settle into a quiet conversation about rest, resources, and what Ryan truly needs.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hey, buddy. There's a bit of fish left from lunch if you want it. Easy now.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: The puppy whimpers, tail tentative, approaches Ryan's outstretched hand.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're so patient with it. I've never seen you sit still this long, just... being quiet with something that's scared.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, well... this one's scared. Don't think it's been treated well before. You can see it in how it moves.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: Takes the fish gently from Ryan's hand, presses against his leg with quiet hunger.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's why, then. You make it safe. That's a gift.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't know how long I can keep this up, honestly. The food supply is getting thin, and we haven't found fresh water in two days.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're doing more than anyone here. Look at it — it already trusts you completely. That takes time to build.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's not about what I want, though. It's just about... keeping things together. All of it. The supplies, the morale, this... situation.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: Finishes eating, curls closer to Ryan's leg, ears relaxing for the first time all day.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That matters more than you know. You matter, Ryan. More than you think.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Does it? Some days I feel like I'm just... burning through everything. My energy, the food, the will to keep pretending it's going to be okay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're not burning out. You're building something. There's a real difference, and I can see it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You think so?

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: Licks his hand slowly, tail moving steady now, the fear draining away with each moment of safety.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know so. I can see it clearly.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The water's gone, by the way. We finished the last of it this morning. And there's maybe a week of food left at this rate.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: There has to be a spring somewhere on this island. Fresh water always finds a way, and so will we.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Have you looked for it yet?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not yet. But tomorrow, first light, I will find it. I promise.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: Yawns, head heavy against Ryan's leg, trusting enough now to let sleep take him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tomorrow. I can believe in that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You should rest tonight. Let someone else watch the fire for once. You're burning yourself out.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The stray won't sleep if I leave it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So let me stay, then. I'll keep the fire, I'll keep watch. You rest and actually recover.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You'd do that? Really, you'd sit here all night?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of course I would. That's what you do for people who matter to you.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: Breathing deep and steady, fully asleep now, the last of the wildness gone, safe in Ryan's presence.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Thank you. I really... thank you, Jade. I didn't know I needed this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Rest now. Tomorrow we find water, and you're going to be okay.

action: Walks down from his tent just as Jade and Ryan settle by the fire, staying at the edge of the light.
thought: She picked him. Again. I watch from the edges.

action: Sits by the fire with the stray dozing against his knee, watching the embers glow steady and warm.
thought: I want to hold this moment. All of it.

action: Arrives with an armful of firewood, settles beside Ryan without hesitation, and feeds the fire a piece at a time.
thought: This feels right. Him, the fire, this peace.

action: Steps out onto the porch as the sun sets, watches the three of them by the fire from the shadows, then turns back inside.
thought: They don't need me there. They have each other.

action: Curls tight against Ryan's leg by the fire, its breathing deep and even, finally resting without flinching.
thought: Safe. He keeps me safe.

description: By the firepit in the fading light, Ryan and Jade settle into warmth while Marcus arrives and the truth spills out—three people caught in a moment neither can hold and none will release.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You came back. I wasn't sure you would after the firewood run.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The light was still beautiful. I couldn't miss it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's better now that you're here. Both of you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fire won't tend itself. Someone should split the wood for tonight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I brought enough. It's stacked by the pit.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I saw. Just saying, it runs low fast.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We can gather more tomorrow. Tonight I want to sit with this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: With what?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: With knowing they're okay. Both of them.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup's okay because you fed it. That's not the same as everything being okay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know that. But it matters.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It does matter. He's right.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Of course it matters. I just don't know how long we can afford to care.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's exactly when we can't afford not to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're not running out yet. Are we?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You haven't been tracking the stores.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then I will.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I will too. We can figure it out together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're by the fire every night now. Both of you. Together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're keeping the fire alive. That's the job.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You could join us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not built for sitting around.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're built for keeping watch. But you're not meant to be alone.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I've been alone my whole life, Ryan. I'm fine with it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't think you are.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You don't know me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm starting to.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then you know I don't do this. I don't sit and talk about feelings.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: This isn't about feelings. It's about dinner. We're all tired.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Exactly. We should be splitting wood, not having a moment.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We can do both.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. You can't. One of you has to give.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm not stepping back, if that's what you're saying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not asking you to. I'm just saying I see what's happening.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And what is that?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That you're falling in. Both of you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And if we are?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then at least one of us is still thinking clearly.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're jealous.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm practical.
